name: Valdeganga desde Mahora — circular por el río Júcar
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Mahora are primarily characterized by routes along the Júcar River, offering a mix of riverside paths and loops through the surrounding landscape. The region features varied terrain suitable for mountain biking, with trails that often incorporate views of the river and local scenery. These routes provide opportunities to explore the area's natural features on two wheels. Best mountain bike trails around Mahora The most popular mountain bike trail is Valdeganga desde Mahora — circular por el río Júcar, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete, following a circular path along the Júcar River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mountainbike loop from Mahora, a moderate 22.4 miles (36.0 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop starting and ending in Mahora. Local mountain bikers also love the Views of the Júcar River – Valdeganga loop from Mahora, a 24.5 miles (39.4 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Mahora is defined by river trails, open landscapes, and local loops. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to more challenging routes. The routes in Mahora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 9 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mahora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Mahora?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Mahora, offering a variety of routes for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored 17 tours in total within the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Mahora?
A: Mountain bike trails in Mahora are primarily characterized by routes along the Júcar River, offering a mix of riverside paths and loops through the surrounding landscape. You can expect varied terrain that often incorporates views of the river and local scenery, with open landscapes and local loops.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Mahora?
A: Yes, Mahora offers some easier mountain bike trails. There are 2 routes classified as 'easy' within the region, providing accessible options for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Mahora?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Mahora has 2 routes classified as 'difficult'. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Trail – Riverside Trail loop from Mahora&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 60 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Mahora?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Mahora are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1241999787/valdeganga-desde-mahora-circular-por-el-rio-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeganga desde Mahora — circular por el río Júcar&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Mahora&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the mountain bike trails in Mahora?
A: While riding in Mahora, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural feature. You might also pass through areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5363840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Square&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4552271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Constitution Square&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses of local life.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails in Mahora that offer views of the Júcar River?
A: Absolutely. The Júcar River is a defining feature of mountain biking in Mahora. Many routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1241999787/valdeganga-desde-mahora-circular-por-el-rio-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeganga desde Mahora — circular por el río Júcar&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33871397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River – Valdeganga loop from Mahora&lt;/a&gt;, are specifically designed to follow or provide scenic overlooks of the river.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Mahora typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Mahora varies depending on the route and your pace. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1241999787/valdeganga-desde-mahora-circular-por-el-rio-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeganga desde Mahora — circular por el río Júcar&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 37 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Mahora&lt;/a&gt; is usually completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Mahora?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 9 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river trails, and the well-planned loops that allow for exploration of the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any specific parking areas for mountain bikers in Mahora?
A: While specific dedicated mountain bike parking facilities are not detailed in the route information, general parking areas are available. One notable highlight is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2826788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Mariquillas&lt;/a&gt;, which is identified as a parking location in the vicinity.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that are suitable for families in Mahora?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' classifications are not provided for all routes, the presence of 'easy' graded trails suggests options suitable for families. These routes tend to have less technical terrain and more manageable distances, making them enjoyable for a wider range of riders.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails in Mahora?
A: The guide data does not specifically list cafes or pubs directly adjacent to the trails. However, as many routes start and end in Mahora, you can expect to find amenities within the town itself, including places to eat and drink after your ride.
tips: Natural pool created by the waters of the Júcar river channel near the upstream reservoir. The crystal clear blue waters are amazing. Dive into a refreshing bath on a warm summer day.
The area is quiet, mountainous and the roads are good for road bikes and the paths are fun for mountain biking.
Ruins of a Franciscan convent on the outskirts of the city of Mahora. The convent was founded in 1611 and closed in March 1836, since it fell into a state of ruin.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Franciscan Convent of Mahora
